# Manifest Lens

Manifest Lens is a local-first web explainer for browser extension manifest.json files.

It helps developers paste, upload, drop, or load a sample browser extension manifest and understand its fields in context. The app renders a source-backed semantic tree and shows explanations for manifest fields, permissions, host permissions, content scripts, background configuration, actions, web accessible resources, content security policy, commands, browser-specific settings, and related extension concepts.

## Primary URL

https://iamaamir.github.io/manifest-lens/

## Source

https://github.com/iamaamir/manifest-lens

## Product scope

Manifest Lens is an explainer-first tool. It is not currently a linter, validator, health score, publishing-readiness checker, automatic fixer, compatibility report generator, or AI-generated explanation tool.

The app runs locally in the browser. Manifest contents are not uploaded to a backend.

## Useful files for agents

- README.md: project overview, setup, validation
- docs/PRD.md: product requirements
- web-extension-manifest-inspector-hld.md: high-level architecture
- docs/roadmap-v1.md: implementation roadmap
- docs/architecture/coding-style.md: architecture and coding style rules
- docs/knowledge/manifest-coverage.md: manifest knowledge coverage matrix
- docs/knowledge/manifest-knowledge-sources.md: reviewed documentation sources
- fixtures/manifests/comprehensive-all-browsers.json: comprehensive sample manifest

## Architecture summary

Manifest Lens uses a TypeScript monorepo with a platform-independent headless core, source-aware JSON parsing, semantic manifest modeling, a static knowledge registry, application state helpers, Web Components UI, and a thin web host.

Package flow:

contracts -> parser-json -> manifest-domain -> knowledge -> core -> application -> ui-components -> host-web -> apps/web

## Local development

Install dependencies:

npm install

Run the web app:

npm run dev --workspace=@manifest-lens/web

Validate:

npm run typecheck
npm run test
npm run build
npm run build --workspace=@manifest-lens/web
npm run e2e

## Important constraints

Do not add remote analysis, runtime AI explanations, diagnostics, automatic fixes, health scores, compatibility reports, CLI behavior, VS Code extension behavior, or browser extension packaging unless explicitly requested for a future phase.

Preserve source text. Do not reserialize user manifests for display.

Keep the core engine platform-independent and serializable.
